title: The Words Of Socrates.
content:
[1]

     A house was built by Socrates
     That failed the public taste to please.
     Some blamed the inside; some, the out; and all
     Agreed that the apartments were too small.
     Such rooms for him, the greatest sage of Greece!
     'I ask,' said he, 'no greater bliss
     Than real friends to fill e'en this.'
     And reason had good Socrates
     To think his house too large for these.
     A crowd to be your friends will claim,
     Till some unhandsome test you bring.
     There's nothing plentier than the name;
     There's nothing rarer than the thing.
meaning:
"The Words Of Socrates." is a quintessential example of Jean de La Fontaine's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
